paragraph1: Cookies are files saved on your phone, tablet or computer when you visit a website. We use cookies to store information about how you use the find a national infrastructure project service, such as the pages you visit.
paragraph2: We use different types of cookies.
paragraph3: You can choose which cookies you're happy for us to use.
paragraph4: We use essential cookies to help you use the find a national infrastructure project service. These do things like remembering your:
paragraph5: These cookies always need to be on.
cookiesPurpose1: Keeps you on the same server, so your user experience stays consistent
cookiesPurpose2: Keeps you on the same server, so your user experience stays consistent
cookiesPurpose3: Keeps you on the same server, so your user experience stays consistent
cookiesPurpose4: Keeps you on the same server, so your user experience stays consistent
cookiesPurpose5: Helps us detect and mitigate malicious bot traffic
cookiesPurpose6: Helps us remember your cookie policy preferences
cookiesPurpose8: Stores your preferred language to view the website
paragraph6: We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the find a national infrastructure project service so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google to use or share the data about how you use this site.
paragraph7: By accepting analytics cookies, you agree to the Planning Inspectorate using your data to help improve the find a national infrastructure project service.
cookiesPurpose9: Helps us count how many people visit our website by tracking if you’ve visited before
cookiesPurpose10: Helps us understand how you use the website by measuring visits and traffic patterns
legend1: Do you want to accept analytics cookies?

successBanner.paragraph1: Government services may set additional cookies and, if so, will have their own cookie policy and banner.
successBanner.linkText1: Go back to the page you were looking at

errorMessage.paragraph1: We use JavaScript to set most of our cookies. Unfortunately Javascript is not running on your browser, so you cannot change your settings. You can try:
errorMessage.listItem2: turning on JavaScript in your browser
